branches.condition: Developer already has an Encore Cloud account
branches.effect_on_first_success: Speeds up onboarding by skipping account creation step
branches.path: Skip account creation prompt and proceed with app creation

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Quick Start Guide: 'If you see this JSON response, you've successfully made an API call to your very first Encore application.'

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer runs the app locally and successfully calls the API endpoint, receiving the expected JSON response
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Receiving the JSON response {"Message": "Hello, world!"} from the API endpoint call
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully run a local Encore app and receive a valid API response from the local development environment
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This milestone marks the first time the developer has a running Encore app locally and can successfully interact with it, demonstrating the platform's core functionality working end-to-end.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has a compatible local development environment (e.g., macOS with Homebrew or equivalent shell environment).
Developer has basic knowledge of TypeScript or Go programming languages.
entry_point.developer_intent: Start building a backend application with Encore by installing the CLI and creating a new app.

friction_gates.description: Creating a free Encore Cloud account is required to manage secrets and enable cloud deployments
Installing the Encore CLI is required to develop and run Encore apps locally

prerequisites.requirement: Install the Encore CLI appropriate for your system (e.g., via Homebrew on macOS)
Create a free Encore Cloud account when prompted during app creation to enable cloud deployment and secret management
Basic familiarity with TypeScript programming language (or Go, but quick start uses TypeScript)

details: Run the appropriate install command for your system, e.g., 'brew install encoredev/tap/encore' on macOS
primary_path.success_signal: Encore CLI command is recognized and runs

details: When prompted, create a free Encore Cloud account
Select TypeScript as the app language
Optionally install AI instructions for enhanced tooling
Provide a name for the app
primary_path.output: New app folder created with starter code
primary_path.success_signal: App folder with starter code is created

primary_path.action: Open and review the starter app code
details: Open 'hello.ts' file in the app folder
Understand the API endpoint definition and service structure
primary_path.success_signal: Developer can identify the API endpoint code

details: Run 'encore run' to start the local development environment
primary_path.output: Local development environment running with infrastructure
primary_path.success_signal: Local environment is up and running without errors
Dashboard shows API explorer and service catalog

details: Use curl or API explorer to call the endpoint, e.g., 'curl http://localhost:4000/hello/world'

surface.name: Encore CLI and local development environment
surface.selection_basis: The CLI is the primary interface for developers to create, run, and manage Encore applications locally and deploy to cloud environments, as documented in the official quick start and overview guides.
